title: Retrieval Review: User-Centered Interface Design
summary: Retrieval Review: User-Centered Interface Design retrieves prior skills in an original changed community-service redesign brief scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply design user goals, design information hierarchy, design spacing alignment, design color typography, design consistency feedback, design prototype test in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real changed community-service redesign brief artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey redesign here. -->

options.text: Changed community-service redesign brief case 2: making every important item larger creates a clearer hierarchy.
Changed community-service redesign brief case 2: rank content and actions from user goals, align structural and visual order, and verify that people locate primary and secondary tasks quickly.
Changed community-service redesign brief case 2: move the primary action visually with CSS while leaving it last in reading order.

options.text: Changed community-service redesign brief case 5: consistency means every component must look identical regardless of purpose.

Changed community-service redesign brief case 5: give every message the same blue treatment so users learn one universal state color.

options.text: Changed community-service redesign brief case 7: the stakeholder feature list is equivalent to evidence about user needs.

options.text: Changed community-service redesign brief case 9: whitespace is unused room that should be filled to maximize value.

Changed community-service redesign brief case 9: choose a unique margin for each element so every section has a custom personality.

options.text: Changed community-service redesign brief case 12: a polished mockup is proof that users can complete the intended task.

Changed community-service redesign brief case 12: ask only whether participants like the colors and treat positive opinions as task success.
